commands.review_summary:
The PR adds numbered file-section rendering across Oracle-generated prompt, browser inline, text bundle, token-stat, export, and test surfaces while preserving raw `createFileSections().sectionText`.

Reproducibility: not applicable. this is a feature PR rather than a bug report. Source inspection confirms current main lacks numbered generated file sections and the PR adds them.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The remaining blocker is a maintainer compatibility decision plus contributor proof, not a safe autonomous repair lane while the public formatter contract is unsettled.

target.title: [codex] Add line numbers to Oracle text bundles
target.body:
## Summary

Oracle-generated text context now carries stable line numbers, so models can cite bundled source as `path:line` or `path:line-line` without guessing whether file citations are accepted. The generated prompt, rendered markdown, browser inline mode, and `attachments-bundle.txt` all use the numbered view, while raw browser uploads, ZIP entries, and legacy `createFileSections().sectionText` stay unmodified for compatibility.

The formatter now has an explicit raw escape hatch via `formatFileSection(..., { lineNumbers: false })`, plus a shared `formatFileSections(...)` helper so generated prompt surfaces do not drift. Token accounting follows the numbered generated view, and the default Oracle system prompt now asks for line-specific file citations when line numbers are present.

## Validation

- `fnm exec --using v24.11.1 pnpm vitest run tests/oracle/markdown.test.ts tests/oracle/oracleExports.test.ts tests/browser/policies.test.ts tests/browser/prompt.test.ts tests/cli/markdownBundle.test.ts tests/cli/runOracle/prompt-and-utils.test.ts` passed: 6 files, 55 tests.
- `fnm exec --using v24.11.1 pnpm run check` passed.
- `git diff --check` passed.
- `fnm exec --using v24.11.1 pnpm test` still has one unrelated SIGINT portability failure in `tests/cli/integrationCli.test.ts`: expected `{ code: 130, signal: null }`, received `{ code: null, signal: "SIGINT" }`; the rest of the suite passed, with 132 files passed and 18 skipped.

## Review

Ran `compound-engineering:ce-code-review` locally and fixed the actionable findings around token-stat assertions, legacy `sectionText` documentation, shared generated-section rendering, API migration notes, and line-specific citation prompting. The local review artifact was written to `/tmp/compound-engineering/ce-code-review/20260603-054114-d9c6521a/review.json`.

---

[![Compound Engineering](https://img.shields.io/badge/Built_with-Compound_Engineering-6366f1)](https://github.com/EveryInc/compound-engineering-plugin)
![Codex](https://img.shields.io/badge/GPT--5.5-000000)
